title: Cirrus: Guarantee $DEST_BRANCH is passed through
body:
There are several runtime contexts (rootless and container) where
`$DEST_BRANCH` is needed but was not supplied.  A prior commit
(c4865767171b) removed the default value, `main` which was being
set incorrectly when CI ran on release branches.  Fix this by ensuring
the variable is non-empty upon entry to `setup_environment.sh`, then
ensure it gets passed through to child environments by way of the
`/etc/ci_environment` file.  This will maintain compatibility with
both CI and `hack/get_ci_vm.sh` use.

Also Ref: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14131

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
The reason I didn't do this in my PR is: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14179/files#diff-f3866c83e5fdf3650df2ad5b9c0c18efaa71891d23675732cc931e90fd2b10faL54-R57

As I read this, it looks like a chicken-egg problem: those variables need to be already set in order to be written out; and, didn't we resolve that DEST_BRANCH is unset in cron and/or branch context?

@edsantiago I'm confused, "The reason I didn't do this in my PR is" link isn't working like I think you intended.

> resolve that DEST_BRANCH is unset in cron and/or branch context?

I don't think so, do you have/find a reference of this happening?  My understanding is if it appears in a top-level `env` definition in `.cirrus.yml` it will be exported to all scripts for all tasks (including cron).  The [issue I found with branches](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14131), was specific to the rootless and containerized contexts.  For them, because this variable was not being written into `/etc/ci_environment` it was never loaded after `ssh` or the `podman` commands.  That caused it to default to `main` (by from `lib.sh` - which you fixed in #14158) - which is clearly wrong for non-main branches.

This PR simply ensures the variable is passed through to all contexts.  Including `hack/get_ci_vm.sh` which makes a point of parsing and stuffing all the right env. vars. into the VM for use.

Does that help answer your questions?

> I'd like to understand the cron/branch question before merging

Absolutely, and maybe I'm forgetting what the problem is/was in that case.

I don't believe there's a chicken/egg problem.  No `contrib/cirrus/*.sh` script should ever be run outside the context of Cirrus-CI or `hack/get_ci_vm.sh`.  In both cases, `.cirrus.yml` is the top-level source-of-truth when it comes to env. vars.  There are some exceptions (`hack/get_ci_vm.sh` can't guess everything), where (when important) we need to set default values in `lib.sh`.

>link isn't working like I think you intended.

Ugh. Thanks, github. That was supposed to link to the 'while ... <passthrough >> /etc/ci_environment` lines, and I think I've answered the question I had (re: chicken/egg relating to how `ci_environment` gets written).

>Does that help answer your questions?

Yes, in the sense that I trust you've given it the thought it needs, thanks. So:

/approve
/lgtm
